candidates.label: Lower-field path clarity v0
candidates.intent: Clarify stage-five lower-field alien paths by slightly lengthening visible dive commitment and reducing lateral ambiguity while preserving missile pace.

promotionGate.read: Candidate is useful evidence but does not clear the measurement gate for runtime promotion.

candidates.label: Lane separation v1
candidates.intent: Reduce confusing lower-field horizontal overlap while leaving dive and shot cadence nearly unchanged.

promotionGate.read: Candidate is useful evidence but does not clear the measurement gate for runtime promotion.

candidates.label: Commitment window v1
candidates.intent: Lengthen the readable lower-field commitment window without spending missile velocity or player single-shot cadence.

promotionGate.read: Candidate clears the measurement gate for browser/contact-sheet review, but it remains analysis-only.

candidates.label: Commitment window v2
candidates.intent: Test a stronger alien-path readability window while preserving enemy missile velocity, enemy missile cadence, and player single-shot cadence exactly.

promotionGate.read: Candidate is useful evidence but does not clear the measurement gate for runtime promotion.

candidates.label: Topology lane offset v1
candidates.intent: Keep the commitment-window improvement but separate lower-field alien lanes from missile spawn lanes by narrowing alien sweep and offsetting shot origin spacing.

promotionGate.read: Candidate is useful evidence but does not clear the measurement gate for runtime promotion.

candidates.label: Topology corridor v2
candidates.intent: Preserve the same missile pace but open a more readable lower-field player corridor through modest dive sweep, sway, and acceleration changes.

promotionGate.read: Candidate is useful evidence but does not clear the measurement gate for runtime promotion.

candidates.label: Topology lane weave v3
candidates.intent: Test a less crowded weave that keeps dive pressure active while reducing low-field missile/dive ambiguity.

promotionGate.read: Candidate is useful evidence but does not clear the measurement gate for runtime promotion.

candidates.label: Threat source corridor v1
candidates.intent: Keep missile pace fixed while avoiding enemy-shot sources that stack directly into the player's crowded lower-field corridor.

promotionGate.read: Candidate is useful evidence but does not clear the measurement gate for runtime promotion.

candidates.label: Threat source separation v2
candidates.intent: Bias enemy-shot sources away from active lower-field dive lanes while keeping the same missile velocity and scheduled cadence.

promotionGate.read: Candidate is useful evidence but does not clear the measurement gate for runtime promotion.

candidates.label: Threat source formation v3
candidates.intent: During crowded lower-field moments, prefer readable formation shot sources instead of stacking shots from diving aliens already occupying the lower field.

promotionGate.read: Candidate is useful evidence but does not clear the measurement gate for runtime promotion.

summary.bestCandidateRead: Commitment window v1 is the best gate-clearing candidate: lower-field readability changes by 0.2/10, routeability by 0.6/10, collision-loss share by -7 points, and measured pressure retention is 104%.
summary.bestStrictReadabilityRead: Threat source corridor v1 has the highest strict readability at 3.6/10, but gate pass is no with collision-loss share 50%.
summary.expandedFamilyRead: Topology lane weave v3 is the best expanded path-topology profile at 0.1/10 readability lift and 1/10 routeability lift; the family did not beat the commitment-window candidate.
summary.threatSourceFamilyRead: Threat source corridor v1 is the best threat-source profile at 0.3/10 readability lift and 0.7/10 routeability lift.
summary.releaseRead: Measured 10 stage-five readability candidates against guardians-stage-five-lower-field-readability-v0. Commitment window v1 is the best gate-clearing profile; Threat source corridor v1 has the highest strict readability but does not clear the gate. Missile pace preserved: yes.

nextSteps: Use the best passing candidate, if any, to generate a browser/contact-sheet before-after review.
Do not promote runtime constants until the candidate also passes stage-five closeness, routeability review, and first-class conformance after refreshed artifacts.
If the threat-source family still does not clear visual review, the next candidate family should combine source eligibility with dive selection fairness before changing missile pace.
